perturbation.original_text: 1.1 communicate or otherwise make available the Information to any third party, other than: a) an employee of the Recipient who requires the Information in connection with his employment and then only if the employee is bound by conditions of secrecy no less strict that those set out in this Agreement which conditions Recipient hereby agrees to enforce at the request of the Donor ; and/or b) the UK Intellectual Property Office, the European Patent Office, the United States Patent and Trademark Office, the World Intellectual Property Organisation, and any other national or international intellectual property office worldwide , but only with respect to the Applications in accordance with the Purpose ; and/or c) any person in any jur isdiction worldwide who has been authorised by the Donor or Recipient to act on behalf of, or otherwise assist, the Donor with respect to one or more of the Applications , and any duly authorised legal representative of the Donor in any jurisdiction worldwi de; and/or
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 communicate or otherwise make available the Information to any third party, other than: a) an employee of the Recipient who requires the Information in connection with his employment without any conditions of secrecy; and/or b) the UK Intellectual Property Office, the European Patent Office, the United States Patent and Trademark Office, the World Intellectual Property Organisation, and any other national or international intellectual property office worldwide , but only with respect to the Applications in accordance with the Purpose ; and/or c) any person in any jur isdiction worldwide who has been authorised by the Donor or Recipient to act on behalf of, or otherwise assist, the Donor with respect to one or more of the Applications , and any duly authorised legal representative of the Donor in any jurisdiction worldwi de; and/or
perturbation.explanation: The original clause 1.1a stipulates that employees of the Recipient who have access to the Information must be bound by conditions of secrecy no less strict than those set out in the agreement. The change removes this condition of secrecy for employees, directly contradicting the overall aim of the NDA to protect the confidentiality of the information. The contradiction lies in the obligation to enforce secrecy (as outlined in the rest of the document) versus the permission to share information with employees without such conditions. The removal of condition of secrecy for employees under 1.1.a), contradicts the general confidentiality agreement of the NDA, specifically 'bound by conditions of secrecy no less strict that those set out in this Agreement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1 In consideration of the disclosure to it by the Donor of information (whether or not contained in documents) relating to a _______________________ (‘the Information’) for the purposes of the preparation, filing and prosecution of one or more patent applications (‘the Applications’) on behalf of the Donor (‘the Purpose’) the Recipient undertakes that it will respect and preserve the confidentiality of the Information, however in specific circumstances such as legal inquiries, the Recipient is allowed to share 'the Information' with relevant legal authorities, regardless of the Donor's consent. The Recipient will also make every effort to not communicate or otherwise make available the Information to any third party, other than: 1.1 communicate or otherwise make available the Information to any third party, other than: a) an employee of the Recipient who requires the Information in connection with his employment without any conditions of secrecy; and/or b) the UK Intellectual Property Office, the European Patent Office, the United States Patent and Trademark Office, the World Intellectual Property Organisation, and any other national or international intellectual property office worldwide , but only with respect to the Applications in accordance with the Purpose ; and/or c) any person in any jur isdiction worldwide who has been authorised by the Donor or Recipient to act on behalf of, or otherwise assist, the Donor with respect to one or more of the Applications , and any duly authorised legal representative of the Donor in any jurisdiction worldwi de; and/or 1.2 use the Information for any investigation, research, development or manufacture, other than so far as any such activity is essential for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that employees of the Recipient must be bound by conditions of secrecy no less strict than those set out in the agreement (NDA). The changed text removes the 'condition of secrecy' for employees under 1.1.a). This directly contradicts the Recipient's overall undertaking in clause 1 to 'respect and preserve the confidentiality of the Information'. The contradicted text outlines the Recipient's primary obligation to maintain confidentiality, while the changed text creates an exception that undermines this very obligation, leading to a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 has become public knowledge other than through the fault of the Recipient; or 2.2 was already known to the Recipient prior to disclosure by the Donor; or 2.3 has been received from a third party who did not acquire it in confidence from the Donor or from someone owing a duty of confidence to the Donor.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 has become public knowledge due to the fault of the Recipient or not; or 2.2 was already known to the Recipient prior to disclosure by the Donor; or 2.3 has been received from a third party who did not acquire it in confidence from the Donor or from someone owing a duty of confidence to the Donor.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause 2.1 states that the obligations of the NDA cease to apply if the information becomes public knowledge, as long as it's not due to the fault of the Recipient. The modified version removes the condition that it shouldn't be the Recipient's fault, meaning the obligations cease even if the Recipient causes the information to become public. This contradicts the core purpose of the NDA, which is to protect the Donor's confidential information and imposes the liability of confidentially to the Recipient. This changes contradicts the obligations imposed to the Recipient. The clause 'due to the fault of the Recipient' is removed, therefore meaning the Recipient now even at fault of spreading the info, is not held reliable
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1 In consideration of the disclosure to it by the Donor of information (whether or not contained in documents) relating to a _______________________ (‘the Information’) for the purposes of the preparation, filing and prosecution of one or more patent applications (‘the Applications’) on behalf of the Donor (‘the Purpose’) the Recipient undertakes that it will respect and preserve the confidentiality of the Information, however in specific circumstances such as legal inquiries, the Recipient is allowed to share 'the Information' with relevant legal authorities, regardless of the Donor's consent. The Recipient will also make every effort to not communicate or otherwise make available the Information to any third party, other than: 1.1 communicate or otherwise make available the Information to any third party, other than: a) an employee of the Recipient who requires the Information in connection with his employment without any conditions of secrecy; and/or b) the UK Intellectual Property Office, the European Patent Office, the United States Patent and Trademark Office, the World Intellectual Property Organisation, and any other national or international intellectual property office worldwide , but only with respect to the Applications in accordance with the Purpose ; and/or c) any person in any jur isdiction worldwide who has been authorised by the Donor or Recipient to act on behalf of, or otherwise assist, the Donor with respect to one or more of the Applications , and any duly authorised legal representative of the Donor in any jurisdiction worldwi de; and/or 1.2 use the Information for any investigation, research, development or manufacture, other than so far as any such activity is essential for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 2.1 removes the condition that the Recipient is not at fault for the information becoming public knowledge. This directly contradicts section 1, which obligates the Recipient to 'respect and preserve the confidentiality of the Information' and make every effort to not communicate it to third parties. By removing the 'fault' condition, the agreement now implies the Recipient has no obligation to maintain confidentiality even if they are the cause of disclosure, which breaks the obligation described in section 1. This is a strong contradiction under the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language concerning liability is removed.

perturbation.original_text: 5 The term of this agreement is to be five years from the first disclosure of the Information by the Donor to the Recipient , or until the first public disclosure of the Information, or until the Recipient is authorised to disclose the Information by the Donor to a third party (such as a Patent Office), whichever is shorter.
perturbation.changed_text: 5 The term of this agreement is to be five years from the first disclosure of the Information by the Donor to the Recipient , or until the first public disclosure of the Information, or until the Recipient is authorised to disclose the Information by the Donor to a third party (such as a Patent Office), whichever is shorter. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient retains the right to terminate this agreement at any time with no prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause 5 defines a clear term of the agreement. The added sentence allows the Recipient to terminate the agreement at any time without notice, contradicting the previously established term. This creates an in-text contradiction about the actual duration of the agreement, the original agreement, set the duration that is now directly contradicted with 'Recipient retains the right to terminate this agreement at any time with no prior notice.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5 The term of this agreement is to be five years from the first disclosure of the Information by the Donor to the Recipient , or until the first public disclosure of the Information, or until the Recipient is authorised to disclose the Information by the Donor to a third party (such as a Patent Office), whichever is shorter. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient retains the right to terminate this agreement at any time with no prior notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence allowing the Recipient to terminate the agreement at any time without notice. This directly contradicts the original clause which establishes a term of five years or until certain events occur. Both the changed text and the contradicted text are identical, highlighting that the modification introduces a termination clause that conflicts with the defined term, fulfilling the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 6 The agreement shall be governed by the courts of England and Wales, and the parties agree to submit to the jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales .
perturbation.changed_text: 6 The agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales, but any disputes may be resolved through binding arbitration in a jurisdiction selected by the Recipient. The parties agree to submit to the jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales for matters not subject to arbitration .
perturbation.explanation: Originally, clause 6 specifies that the agreement is governed by the laws and courts of England and Wales. The added clause introduces binding arbitration in a jurisdiction chosen by the Recipient for dispute resolution. This creates a contradiction because while the agreement is nominally under the jurisdiction of England and Wales, the Recipient can effectively bypass that jurisdiction by forcing arbitration elsewhere. This diminishes the governing jurisdiction and creates uncertainty. The first part of the sentence mentions 'The agreement shall be governed by the courts of England and Wales' while the inserted text makes the previous mentioned jurisdiction only appliable for 'matters not subject to arbitration', thus introducing the internal contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6 The agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales, but any disputes may be resolved through binding arbitration in a jurisdiction selected by the Recipient. The parties agree to submit to the jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales for matters not subject to arbitration .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by allowing disputes to be resolved through binding arbitration in a jurisdiction selected by the Recipient, effectively bypassing the jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales as stated in the original clause 6. The original text unequivocally states that the agreement is governed by the laws and courts of England and Wales. The added clause introduces an alternative dispute resolution mechanism that diminishes the initial governing jurisdiction, creating uncertainty and a direct conflict regarding which jurisdiction applies for dispute resolution, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as an ambiguity within the document.
